gold_texts: ["The picture is very clear.  It is myxoid cyst.", "No.  This is cutaneous cysts at finger.  Need surgery + pathological testing.", "Impression: myxoid cyst. On the side, near the end of the thumb, there is a semi-spherical cyst 1 cm X 1 cm.  Color is normal, surface is smooth, shiny, soft and has the feeling of being a cyst. (After it is poked open, you should see sticky but  transparent liquid inside.  This may happen repeatedly). This disease mostly is caused by bruising or under pressure for an extended period of time.  Treatment: fill in small amount of triamcinolone acetonide after draining the sticky liquid inside.  But it may relapse.  The best way to handle it is to remove it surgically."]

candidate: Likely chronic hand eczema, possibly irritant contact dermatitis. Patch testing recommended.  Avoid irritants.  See dermatologist.
query_text: Related to Eczema, pompholyx.  Request for diagnosis. Suffering eczema and epompholyx at hands from the year of 2010.  It is attacking me recurrently.  Very painful.  Hope that physicians from all places could take a look.  Following is the history:-  Work History:  Graduated in June, 2009.  Start working after that.  Work requires contact with  hydrochloric acid with low concentration inside a trench.  It is used to clean the oxidized surface of metal.  There is no use of protective gloves durig the process, and has direct contact with the hydrochloric acid.  Both hands are involved in the physical work, have direct contact with industrial grease.  Change of work place in March 2010.  Working in the office there, with occasional contact with metal.  Not sure it is caused by allergy with metal.  Symptoms:  Blisters on the sides of both palms.  Very itchy.  If broken after scratching, liquid of light yellow in color is flowing out.  The more I scratch, the more is the itching.  Scab is forming after lesions.  Skin turns hard and starts shredding.  Mostly happening in Summer;  less serious in Winter.  Medical History: 1.  Diagnosis as eczema and epompholyx at the Dermatology Department of a local hospital.  Treatment is:  soaking in diluted Aluminum acetate solution, and topical use of Triamcinolone Acetonide Acetate and Miconazole Nitrate Neomycin Sulfate Cream.  No improvement after using them. 2. Soaking in salicylic acid solution by myself.  Effect is not obvious.  Have tried that 5 times. 3.  Topical use of Ketoconazole Ointment by myself.  Effect is not obvious. 4.  Diagnosis as epompholyx at a county level hospital. Treatment: Soaking in boric acid solution; oral use of Loratadine and XTR (Xiatare) tablets; Topical use of Urea ointment for one week.  After that change to another antifungal ointment which I don't recall the name. Also prescribed by the physician at the time is Compound Camphor Cream for use when itchy.  With all these, I don't feel itchy at all.  Even if I drink alcohol, the itchiness is still very minimal.  Unfortunately the problem comes back afte 6 months. 5. Soaking in Zuguang Powder+Pikangwang (A chinese herbal medicine containing Ketoconazole) every other night.  Yesterday was the 4 times.  The symptom is relieving a bit.  Skin is shredding, and becoming dry.  Topical use of Pikangwang is not very effective.  Each of the treatment and use of medications mentioned above is spaced at least 2 months apart.  I would be very grateful if you guys can offer any help.  The disease is so troublesome to me. I smoke, and not particularly fond of drinking alcohol (except when I accompany my boss on events).  I am fond of eating spicy food.  However, because of the problem on my hands, I have cut back most of it.  The pictures below are downloaded from the internet.  My situations are quite similar to them.  My skin is thicker, and the area of skin shredding is also bigger as well.  When the weather is warm, I can feel there are numerous blisters below the skin.  It is itchy, more so if I scratch it.
gold_texts: ["Eczema", "Should be eczema at the hands.", "The main thing is to avoid contact with alkaline solutions.  Otherwise problem would be coming back.", "Eczema is very stubborn.  Apart from the use of topical ointment, use of chinese herbal medicine to help conditioning of the body should be considered.", "Oral administration:  Pill prepared from Saposhnikovia divaricata + pill prepared from chinese angelica and Sophora flavescens (or Sophora flavescens tablets).  External use: Soaking in boiled water with 10g each of the following herbal medicines: Sophora flavescens, perfoliate knotweed, Bidens bipinnata, Vitiligo skin, hyssop,  and spina gleditsiae, After that topical use of Shufuda (A chinese over the counter herbal drug to help relieve problem of skin)"]

The history of contact with hydrochloric acid and industrial grease, as well as the presence of blisters and itching, suggest that this may be an occupational-related eczema. It is important to continue with the current treatment and to work with a dermatologist to develop a comprehensive treatment plan.
query_text: Related to Eczema, pompholyx.  Request for diagnosis. Suffering eczema and epompholyx at hands from the year of 2010.  It is attacking me recurrently.  Very painful.  Hope that physicians from all places could take a look.  Following is the history:-  Work History:  Graduated in June, 2009.  Start working after that.  Work requires contact with  hydrochloric acid with low concentration inside a trench.  It is used to clean the oxidized surface of metal.  There is no use of protective gloves durig the process, and has direct contact with the hydrochloric acid.  Both hands are involved in the physical work, have direct contact with industrial grease.  Change of work place in March 2010.  Working in the office there, with occasional contact with metal.  Not sure it is caused by allergy with metal.  Symptoms:  Blisters on the sides of both palms.  Very itchy.  If broken after scratching, liquid of light yellow in color is flowing out.  The more I scratch, the more is the itching.  Scab is forming after lesions.  Skin turns hard and starts shredding.  Mostly happening in Summer;  less serious in Winter.  Medical History: 1.  Diagnosis as eczema and epompholyx at the Dermatology Department of a local hospital.  Treatment is:  soaking in diluted Aluminum acetate solution, and topical use of Triamcinolone Acetonide Acetate and Miconazole Nitrate Neomycin Sulfate Cream.  No improvement after using them. 2. Soaking in salicylic acid solution by myself.  Effect is not obvious.  Have tried that 5 times. 3.  Topical use of Ketoconazole Ointment by myself.  Effect is not obvious. 4.  Diagnosis as epompholyx at a county level hospital. Treatment: Soaking in boric acid solution; oral use of Loratadine and XTR (Xiatare) tablets; Topical use of Urea ointment for one week.  After that change to another antifungal ointment which I don't recall the name. Also prescribed by the physician at the time is Compound Camphor Cream for use when itchy.  With all these, I don't feel itchy at all.  Even if I drink alcohol, the itchiness is still very minimal.  Unfortunately the problem comes back afte 6 months. 5. Soaking in Zuguang Powder+Pikangwang (A chinese herbal medicine containing Ketoconazole) every other night.  Yesterday was the 4 times.  The symptom is relieving a bit.  Skin is shredding, and becoming dry.  Topical use of Pikangwang is not very effective.  Each of the treatment and use of medications mentioned above is spaced at least 2 months apart.  I would be very grateful if you guys can offer any help.  The disease is so troublesome to me. I smoke, and not particularly fond of drinking alcohol (except when I accompany my boss on events).  I am fond of eating spicy food.  However, because of the problem on my hands, I have cut back most of it.  The pictures below are downloaded from the internet.  My situations are quite similar to them.  My skin is thicker, and the area of skin shredding is also bigger as well.  When the weather is warm, I can feel there are numerous blisters below the skin.  It is itchy, more so if I scratch it.

candidate: Possibly tinea cruris.  See dermatologist; inadequate response suggests alternative diagnosis.  Combination therapy acceptable.
query_text: Would expert please help.  Is this tinea cruris? Have been to two hospitals. First one said that it is fungal infection.  Second one said it is tinea cruris. The rashes have come out for almost a month.    Before I went to the hospital, I used ketoconazole ointment topically, and there was seeminly some improvement.  However, the number of rashes were increasing later on.  After I went to the hospital, I started to use bifonazole solution.  But it is not under control.  From the picture, it can be seen the rashes are increasing in size.  With that, I would like expert here to determine whether it is fungal infection or tinea cruris.  If that is the case, why was the medication unable to put it under control, and it is growing and spreading out.  On the surface, there is no leisons, not itchy, not painful.  If that is not the case, what is the problem?  There is no other symptom, no itchiness, no pain, but the number of rashes is increasing and growing bigger.  How long would it take when using antifungal drug to make it effective.  The physician prescribed bifonazole spray and terbinafine hydrochloride cream.  First use the spray and then apply the cream at the problem area.  Is this OK?
gold_texts: ["These two medicines are relatively good to treat tinea cruris.  Should continue to use the drugs for more than 2 weeks, or to continue to use them 1 to 2  weeks after the skin leisons are healing.   This is to avoid recurrence of the disease.", "Suggest to perform a pathological test for fungal infection.  I don't feel it is tinea cruris.", "I don't feel it is tinea cruris.", "Eczema?  Dermatitis?", "Impression: tinea cruris Treatment: terbinafine hydrochloride cream, topical use (must complete the whole treatment cycle)", "Diagnosis to determine to disease:  Should distinguish among erythrasma, seborrheic dermatitis, Proliferative pemphigus,  and Erosive Psoriasis.  5. Treatment:  Obese people should bath regularly and use drying powder to dry up the body.  Treatment should be the same as having tinea corporis.  Because skin at the groin area is very delicate, should not use strong drug to affect the skin.  Generally should use Resorcinol cream or 1% Econazole or Clotrimazole cream, 2 % miconazole, bifonazole, ketoconazole, sertafurazole, butenafine, terbinafine cream are also O.K.   Orally, the use of Flavomycin is rather effective.  But it could easily relapse after stopping the medication.  So it is not recommended. Fluconazol, Itraconazole, Terbinafine etc may be used in short term. Cause of the disease Commonly caused by flocculent epidermal tinea, trichophyton, and red trichophyton.  Sometimes, invasion to the groin area by Candida albicans resulting in red scaling of skin.  May have papulovesicle at the edge.  This disease can happen easily in the humid season, mostly with male who are sweating a lot.  Workers in specific disciplines  such as drivers and people who are in sitting position for extended period of time could contract this disease easily.  2. clinical manifestation Often occurring at the skin opposite to the scrotum, on one side or both.  Mostly exhibiting in a circle or semi-circle rash.  Initially growing as a small patch of rash on the inside of the upper part of the buttock.  There is scaling and spreading to all sides.  The border / edge is rather clear.  On top of it, it has blisters, lumps, and scab.  The central part of it can be healing by itself with scaling and pigment decomposition.  After some time, part of the skin could be thickening and lichenified, along with itchiness.  In serious cases, it can spread to the inner side of the buttock, perineum and its surroundings.  The lower side of rash is rather clear.  Sometimes, it could also affect the scrotum and its root area.  3. Diagnosis:  It can be diagnosed based on clinical manifestation and test on fungal infection.  Diagnosis to determine to disease should distinguish among erythrasma, seborrheic dermatitis, Proliferative pemphigus,  and Erosive Psoriasis.  Definition of Fungus  Fungus is a living creature with real nucleus.  The most common fungus is the mushrooms.  In addition, it also includes Mold and yeast.  There are more than 70,000 kinds of fungus.  It is believed that this only constitutes a small part of the fungus. Originally most fungus is classified as plant or animal. At present, fungus has been classified separately from animal or plant, and forms its own realm in parallel with Animal, Plant, Prokaryotic and Protobiotic Realms. It has a strong cell wall and a real nucleus.  It does not have chlorophyll and is heterotrophic.  Typically, it can be reproduced asexually and sexually, producing spores in various forms.  Based on the characteristics of its growth and difference in physical appearances, it can be separated as yeast, fungus and mushrooms.  Among them, 300 or so could cause diseases. With the exception of Cryptococcus and mushrooms, most of the disease causing fungi are mold medically.  Based on the locations of human body being invaded by fungus, clinically it can be separated into superficial fungal infection and deep fungal infection. Fungal enteritis is a deep fungal infection. Superficial fungal infection invades skin, hair and nail.  Deep fungal infection can invade body skin, mucosa, deep tissues and internal organs, and may result in disseminated hyperinfection of the whole body. Deep fungal infection at the intestines is fungal enteritis. May also appear in babies as candida enteritis, or fungal infection to the whole body, such as Histoplasmosis being a complication with AIDS.", "perform a test on fungal infection.", "Not tinea cruris.  That would be itchy.  Part of the skin mucosa is changing to whitish in color.  Should not appear in the form of rash physiologically.  It looks more like eczema. Recommend to perform a culture test on bacteria.  If it is tinea cruris, may use nystatin orally."]

Generally should use Resorcinol cream or 1% Econazole or Clotrimazole cream, 2 % miconazole, bifonazole, ketoconazole, sertafurazole, butenafine, terbinafine cream are also O.K.   Orally, the use of Flavomycin is rather effective.  But it could easily relapse after stopping the medication.  So it is not recommended. Fluconazol, Itraconazole, Terbinafine etc may be used in short term. Cause of the disease Commonly caused by flocculent epidermal tinea, trichophyton, and red trichophyton.  Sometimes, invasion to the groin area by Candida albicans resulting in red scaling of skin.  May have papulovesicle at the edge.  This disease can happen easily in the humid season, mostly with male who are sweating a lot.  Workers in specific disciplines  such as drivers and people who are in sitting position for extended period of time could contract this disease easily.  2. clinical manifestation Often occurring at the skin opposite to the scrotum, on one side or both.  Mostly exhibiting in a circle or semi-circle rash.  Initially growing as a small patch of rash on the inside of the upper part of the buttock.  There is scaling and spreading to all sides.  The border / edge is rather clear.  On top of it, it has blisters, lumps, and scab.  The central part of it can be healing by itself with scaling and pigment decomposition.  After some time, part of the skin could be thickening and lichenified, along with itchiness.  In serious cases, it can spread to the inner side of the buttock, perineum and its surroundings.  The lower side of rash is rather clear.  Sometimes, it could also affect the scrotum and its root area.  3. Diagnosis:  It can be diagnosed based on clinical manifestation and test on fungal infection. Based on the characteristics of its growth and difference in physical appearances, it can be separated as yeast, fungus and mushrooms.  Among them, 300 or so could cause diseases. With the exception of Cryptococcus and mushrooms, most of the disease causing fungi are mold medically.  Based on the locations of human body being invaded by fungus, clinically it can be separated into superficial fungal infection and deep fungal infection. Fungal enteritis is a deep fungal infection. Superficial fungal infection invades skin, hair and nail.  Deep fungal infection can invade body skin, mucosa, deep tissues and internal organs, and may result in disseminated hyperinfection of the whole body. query_text: Sore at the External auditory canal, getting scabbed with discharge. It started first as scoring at the external auditory canal when cleaning / picking the ear.  It was summer back then. With repeated scratching by fingers, the scab comes off, then starts bleeding.  It has been one year like this, happening recurrently and causing a lot of inconvenience to my daily life. With the use of Halcinonide Solution again for half a month, the problem is gone, but relapses a week after stopping the solution.  This repeats again after use of another 2 months.  Now I am at a loss, and don’t know how to deal with it.  Would like to ask experts for help.

gold_texts: ["It is likely due to bleeding underneath the nail. query_text: I had a burn from the day before yesterday afternoon. Now there is blister. I popped the blister this morning, as shown in the image. candidate: If your last tetanus booster was within 10 years, you likely don't need another one.  If it's been longer, or if you're unsure, it's best to consult a doctor or visit an urgent care facility to ensure adequate protection.  Clean the wound thoroughly with soap and water.
query_text: I scraped my leg on a rusty railing while playing football at 5 PM, resulting in a wound that bled. I applied iodine when I got home at 7:30 PM, as shown in the picture. Should I get a tetanus shot in this situation?
gold_texts: ["Recommend tetanus shot.", "Wound appears to be a superficial abrasion/laceration.  Would recommend doing local wound care including avoiding drying agents such as alcohol, hydroperoxide, or iodine as these should not be used on open skin.  Instead, use a topical antibiotic ointment and cover with a nonadherent dressing.  Tetanus shot should be updated if it is not up-to-date."]

I would recommend that you go to the nearest hospital to have this performed if you are concerned about a fracture.", "An x-ray is the best way for us to determine whether there is an underlying fracture or bony damage. I would recommend being seen by a physician in person."]

candidate: The nail may regrow, but damage to the nail matrix can cause permanent deformity.  Self-treating can increase infection risk. Please see a doctor to evaluate for nail bed injury, infection, and possible nail removal.  Do not attempt further drainage yourself.
query_text: Will it still grow nail I was slammed by the door pretty hard. There was bruises in the bottom half immediatly. The bruising expanded to everywhere the second and third day. The third day was very distended, and I let out some of the blood myself for three times. It then no longer appeared distended. It has been 10ish days, and it no longer hurt. However, the nail does not grow any more, and the nail is sticking to the blood underneath it, and then the nail had separate from the skin around it. I have clipped the top part of the nail that was separating from the skin. The nail on the side and on the back seems intact with the skin. My nail typically grows very quick, but this have been ten days without growth. Will it stop growing? What should I do.
gold_texts: ["The nail will grow back but it may take up to 6 months.", "There appears to be a underlying hematoma underneath your nail. I would recommend being seen by a physician to have your nail removed and to prevent or inflammation to your nail"]

Continue performing wound care by washing the area with soap and water, applying antibiotic ointment and cover with a bandage. Consult a doctor if you have fever, chills, warmth, swelling, pain, redness, bleeding or pus-like discharge.", "I would recommend keeping the area clean and dry, applying a non-adhesive dressing to the wound as well as bacitracin ointment."]

candidate: I'm sorry to hear about your injury. Iodine can help prevent infection and promote healing, but it won't necessarily speed up the healing process. It's important to keep the wound clean and dry, and to avoid activities that could further injure it. If the wound is deep or won't heal after a few weeks, it's a good idea to see a doctor for further evaluation and treatment.

query_text: It's healed but my hand is still a bit swollen. How can I reduce the swelling?
gold_texts: ["You can take over the counter anti-inflammation medicine, elevate the hand and/or apply an ACE wrap to help reduce the swelling.", "Appears to be a laceration to the webspace between the first and second digits status post suture repair. In regards to the clinical question of persistent swelling, as long as you have been evaluated for any underlying bony injury, some degree of swelling is expected and will take time to resolve.  You can speed up swelling resolution by keeping the affected extremity elevated, applying ice, and gentle compression.  Additional gentle range of motion exercises and skin stretching will help the affected area."]

candidate: Go to the ER immediately.  This requires X-rays to rule out fractures.  Persistent pain and swelling suggest a significant injury. You need professional medical evaluation.
query_text: Help, my feet is having intolerable pain. My feet had been hit by a car. Help, my feet is having intolerable pain. My feet had been hit by a car, then became swollen. There was bruises in the early phase, now the bruise had been mostly resolved. It is still swollen, and I can't leave my leg down.
gold_texts: ["I would recommend that you see a Podiatry specialist for further evaluation. In the meantime, you can take anti-inflammatory medication to help with the pain and swelling if not already doing so. Continue applying ice packs, wearing compression socks or use an ACE wrap and elevating the foot.", "I would recommend being seen by a physician in person to determine what is causing the swelling. It is possible you have a underlying fracture, and it is important to evaluate for a blood clot given the swelling. ￼"]

query_text: My finger was hit by the electric fan, and the tissue was everted out I did some compression to push the tissue back inside. The outter layer had grown back together, but a lump had formed, and it is changing color. What should I do, is it infected. Pleas help.

candidate: Infected wounds often scar. Healing time varies based on depth and extent of infection, but can range from a few weeks to several months. Follow your doctor's instructions for wound care, including prescribed antibiotics, and keep the wound clean and covered.
query_text: My wound is infected Picture one is taken right after the fall. The day after the fall, I painted erythromycin ointment at the scarring site, and the ointment might have caused the wound to close. The second image is taken on the fifth day. When I use a q tip to press on the wound, yellow pus will drain out from the wound. Realizing that this does not look right, I went to the hospital today and the doctor peeled off half of the scar and the other half will be peeled off tomorrow. It hurts, will it leave a scar after this is gets peel off? How many days does it take to heal?
gold_texts: ["likely will leave a scar, will take up to 8 weeks to heal", "Fairly large soft tissue injury with scab formation.  Would not recommend removing any further scab as this is part of the healing process.  Wound does not appear to be secondarily infected.  Would recommend local wound care with topical antibiotic ointment, nonadherent dressing such as Xeroform or Adaptic and changing the dressing daily.  During daily dressing changes, gently cleanse the wound with warm soapy water.  Avoid any drying agents including alcohol, hydrogen peroxide, or iodine."]

query_text: Fell off my bike a few days ago. Bought ointment and applied it myself. Not sure what to do. Should I wrap it in gauze? I've heard it heals slower with gauze. Also, what medication should I apply?
gold_texts: ["Continue performing wound care with washing with soap and water, applying antibiotic ointment and cover with a bandage. No surrounding erythema or significant drainage/bleeding on the included picture.  Does not appear to be amenable for suture repair.  Would recommend local wound care including thorough cleaning with warm soapy water, application of topical antibiotic ointment and coverage with nonadherent dressing.  Dressing should be changed daily and during dressing changes gently cleanse the wound with warm soapy water.  Avoid any drying agents such as hydrogen peroxide, alcohol, or iodine to open skin."]

gold_texts: ["湿疹与皮炎原本很难区分，一般认为病因比较清楚者可径称什么皮炎如接触性皮炎、药物性皮炎、神经性皮炎等，若病因不清则称为湿疹。这个病人除外用药物以外，反复发作可全身系统用药。", "可考虑玫瑰糠疹", "看皮疹大多边界不清，也不知有没有薄膜现象及露滴现象，脱屑也不像银屑病的鳞屑。还是考虑湿疹类。", "玫瑰糠疹/////////", "像银屑病，抠一个皮损！！！！", "此皮损向四周扩散，四周有高起性皮损， 此皮损符合体癣表现。", "考虑湿疹。", "单从皮疹形态及分布部位考虑，像湿疹，外涂艾洛松，注意尽量不要搔抓，可同时应用医用护肤品修复皮肤屏障，如果无明显改善，应做皮肤病例以进一步确诊。"]

gold_texts: ["这两种药都是治疗股癣比较好的药了  要坚持使用2周以上或是皮损消退在继续使用1--2周以免复发", "去做个真菌刮片吧，感觉不象股癣", "觉得不像股藓", "湿疹？皮炎？", "印象：股癣 治疗：盐酸特比萘芬乳膏 外用（一定要足疗程）", "鉴别诊断 应与红癣、脂溢性皮炎、增殖性天疱疮、糜烂性银屑病等相鉴别。 5治疗 肥胖者应常洗澡并敷以扑粉等。 治疗同体癣。由于阴股部的皮肤较娇嫩，应注意勿用过于**的药水，以免**皮肤，一般用复方间苯二酚涂剂或1%益康唑或克霉唑霜，2%咪康唑、联苯苄唑、酮康唑、舍他糠唑、布替萘芬、特比奈芬霜也可用之。 口服灰黄霉素有一定疗效，但停药后易复发，不宜使用。氟康唑、伊曲康唑、特比奈芬等可短期使用。 病因 常由絮状表皮癣菌、须癣毛癣菌、红色毛癣菌等引起，有时，白色念珠菌也好侵犯腹股沟部位而呈红斑脱屑性斑片，其边缘可有丘疱疹。 本病在温热潮湿的季节容易发生，男性多汗者尤易发病。特殊工种如汽车司机、长期坐位者也易发病。 2临床表现 常发生于阴囊对侧的大腿皮肤，一侧或双侧，多呈环状或半环状斑片。初于股上部内侧出现小片红斑，其上有脱屑，并逐渐扩展而向四周蔓延，边界清楚，其上有丘疹、水疱、结痂。中央部位可自愈，有色素沉着或脱屑，历久则于局部皮肤发生浸润增厚呈苔藓化，常伴痒感。严重者常扩展波及股内侧、会阴或**周围，其下缘多清晰。有时尚可波及阴囊、**根部等处。 3诊断 根据临床表现、皮损形态及真菌学检查可确诊。 鉴别诊断 应与红癣、脂溢性皮炎、增殖性天疱疮、糜烂性银屑病等相鉴别。 5治疗 肥胖者应常洗澡并敷以扑粉等。 治疗同体癣。由于阴股部的皮肤较娇嫩，应注意勿用过于**的药水，以免**皮肤，一般用复方间苯二酚涂剂或1%益康唑或克霉唑霜，2%咪康唑、联苯苄唑、酮康唑、舍他糠唑、布替萘芬、特比奈芬霜也可用之。 口服灰黄霉素有一定疗效，但停药后易复发，不宜使用。氟康唑、伊曲康唑、特比奈芬等可短期使用。 真菌感染定义： 真菌（Fungus）是一种真核生物。最常见的真菌是各类蕈类，另外真菌也包括霉菌和酵母。现在已经发现了七万多种真菌，估计只是所有存在的一小半。大多真菌原先被分为动物或植物。 目前，真菌在分类学上已***为界，与动物界、植物界、原核生物界和原生生物界平行。真菌具有坚固的细胞壁和真正的细胞核，不含叶绿素，是异养性的，以寄生或腐生方式生存，典型者兼有有性生殖和无性生殖，产生各种形态的孢子。根据生长特性与形态差异，可将真菌简单分为酵母、真菌和蕈(蘑菇)。其中对人类有致病性的真菌约有300多个种类。除新型隐球菌和蕈外，医学上有意义的致病性真菌几乎都是霉菌。根据侵犯人体部位的不同，临床上将致病真菌分为浅部真菌和深部真菌。真菌性肠炎即属于深部真菌病。　浅部真菌(癣菌)仅侵犯皮肤、毛发和指(趾)甲，而深部真菌能侵犯人体皮肤、黏膜、深部组织和内脏，甚至引起全身播散性感染。深部真菌感染肠道即表现为真菌性肠炎，可***存在如婴儿念珠菌肠炎，或为全身性真菌感染的表现之一，如艾滋病并发播散性组织胞浆菌病", "做个真菌刮片", "不是股藓，股藓要痒，局部皮肤粘膜有白色粘膜样改变，一般不会出现疹子一样的病理，倒像是湿疹，做个细菌培养看看，如果是股藓可以配合制霉菌素口服。"]

query_text: 关于湿疹、汗疱疹，求诊断 手上湿疹、汗疱疹从10年至今，反复发作，痛苦啊，望各路医生给看看。以下是记录： 工作历程：09年6月毕业，之后工作，因工作需要接触低浓度盐酸，盐酸在槽内，用来洗去金属表面铁锈，操作过程无手套等保护，直接接触盐酸，同时双手参与体力活动，经常直接接触工业油污。10年3月后换单位，一直在办公室，偶尔接 触金属，不好判断是否金属过敏引起。 病症：双手手掌侧皮层内有泡，很痒，抓破后流出谈黄色液体，越抓越痒，皮损后结壳，皮肤变硬、脱屑。夏天多发、冬季减缓。 用药记录： 1、地级市皮肤医院诊断为湿疹、汗疱疹，用药醋酸铝粉末冲调后泡后，外用曲咪新乳膏，用后效果不明显。 2、自用药水杨酸浸泡，效果不明显，类似用药记录5。 3、自用复方酮康唑软膏，效果不明显。 4、县级市一医院诊断汗疱疹，用药硼酸浸泡、内服氯雷他定和百癣夏塔热片、外用尿素软膏一周后改用一种软膏，具体忘记了，好像是抗真菌的，同时医生开有复方樟脑乳膏，说痒的时候用。这一套下来之后不痒了，连喝酒之后都只有轻微的痒。不过半年后复发。 5、自用足光散+皮康王。足光散浸泡间隔一晚一次，昨天浸泡第4次。浸泡后痒的症状减轻了许多，手上脱皮、干燥，外用皮康王。效果感觉也不是很理想。 以上用药记录至少互相间隔2各月以上。各位大神，有没有好点的方法，跪求了。这病好烦人哎。 本人抽烟，不喜酒（陪领导的时候是没办法），爱吃各种**、辛辣，因为双手这样，也忌口了很多，哎。 以下图片是网上找到的，跟我手上差不多，我手上厚皮、脱屑面积比他大一些，天热的时候摸明显摸到皮下有很多泡，有点痒，越抓越痒的那种。

query_text: 关于湿疹、汗疱疹，求诊断 手上湿疹、汗疱疹从10年至今，反复发作，痛苦啊，望各路医生给看看。以下是记录： 工作历程：09年6月毕业，之后工作，因工作需要接触低浓度盐酸，盐酸在槽内，用来洗去金属表面铁锈，操作过程无手套等保护，直接接触盐酸，同时双手参与体力活动，经常直接接触工业油污。10年3月后换单位，一直在办公室，偶尔接 触金属，不好判断是否金属过敏引起。 病症：双手手掌侧皮层内有泡，很痒，抓破后流出谈黄色液体，越抓越痒，皮损后结壳，皮肤变硬、脱屑。夏天多发、冬季减缓。 用药记录： 1、地级市皮肤医院诊断为湿疹、汗疱疹，用药醋酸铝粉末冲调后泡后，外用曲咪新乳膏，用后效果不明显。 2、自用药水杨酸浸泡，效果不明显，类似用药记录5。 3、自用复方酮康唑软膏，效果不明显。 4、县级市一医院诊断汗疱疹，用药硼酸浸泡、内服氯雷他定和百癣夏塔热片、外用尿素软膏一周后改用一种软膏，具体忘记了，好像是抗真菌的，同时医生开有复方樟脑乳膏，说痒的时候用。这一套下来之后不痒了，连喝酒之后都只有轻微的痒。不过半年后复发。 5、自用足光散+皮康王。足光散浸泡间隔一晚一次，昨天浸泡第4次。浸泡后痒的症状减轻了许多，手上脱皮、干燥，外用皮康王。效果感觉也不是很理想。 以上用药记录至少互相间隔2各月以上。各位大神，有没有好点的方法，跪求了。这病好烦人哎。 本人抽烟，不喜酒（陪领导的时候是没办法），爱吃各种**、辛辣，因为双手这样，也忌口了很多，哎。 以下图片是网上找到的，跟我手上差不多，我手上厚皮、脱屑面积比他大一些，天热的时候摸明显摸到皮下有很多泡，有点痒，越抓越痒的那种。
